evals.prompt: Design an SMS appointment-reminder program for a primary care clinic. We have a 22% no-show rate and want to reduce it.
evals.expected_output: Should check for .agents/healthcare-context.md first. Should produce a reminder design with: cadence (typically 7-day, 72-hour, 24-hour touchpoints), timing aligned to the action window, frequency caps, quiet hours, and an escalation rail (SMS to call to mail). Should address HIPAA + TCPA posture: treatment-message classification, prior express consent (not necessarily written) for treatment SMS to a wireless number, BAA on the messaging vendor (not just conduit), telling patients SMS is not fully secure. Should propose channel-level opt-out (STOP) handling. Should propose measurement with a control or comparison cohort to attribute no-show reduction, with equity stratification by language, age, and SDOH. Should call out 'verify current FCC rules and any recent orders' for TCPA specifics. Should mention Section 1557 language access.
assertions: Checks for healthcare-context.md
Proposes multi-touch cadence aligned to action window
Addresses HIPAA + TCPA posture explicitly
Notes BAA requirement (conduit exception is narrow)
Includes STOP / opt-out handling
Proposes control / comparison cohort for measurement
Stratifies measurement by equity factors
References Section 1557 language access
Includes verify caveat for current regulations

evals.prompt: We want to launch a post-discharge follow-up program for CHF patients to reduce 30-day readmissions. What should the program look like?
evals.expected_output: Should propose a CHF-specific archetype: 24-48 hour callback, daily weight check, symptom check-in (often via SMS or app), medication reconciliation, follow-up booking, red-flag triage, 7-day and 30-day touchpoints. Should select an appropriate behavior-change framework (e.g., Health Belief Model or COM-B for self-care, BJ Fogg for daily-weight habit). Should match channels to a Medicare-leaning, older-adult population (consider IVR / human callback, mailed letters alongside SMS; address accessibility). Should plan measurement with a comparison cohort (matched controls or pre/post-with-controls), measuring readmission as primary, with reach/response/action intermediate metrics, equity stratification, and PMPM impact. Should call out HIPAA / BAA, accessibility, and language access.
assertions: Proposes CHF-specific touchpoint pattern
Selects an appropriate behavior-change framework with rationale
Matches channels to older-adult / Medicare audience including IVR or callback
Plans measurement with comparison cohort, not pre/post only
Includes equity stratification
Addresses HIPAA + BAA
Addresses accessibility and language access

evals.prompt: help us think through patient engagement for our medicaid plan. we have a lot of churn and low preventive screening rates.
evals.expected_output: Should trigger on casual phrasing. Should recognize Medicaid-specific constraints: high digital-divide risk, address volatility, language diversity, distrust, churn cycles tied to redetermination. Should propose channel mix that does not require smartphone + broadband by default (SMS + mail + IVR + community-health worker outreach). Should address SDOH screening and closed-loop referral (PRAPARE / AHC-HRSN; Unite Us / Findhelp — with verify caveats). Should call out 42 CFR Part 2 for any SUD-touching outreach and state-specific Medicaid managed-care rules. Should propose preventive screening outreach by birthday month or care-gap trigger, with equity stratification baked in. Should suggest measurement with a comparison cohort and warn that churn analysis is biased by enrollment volatility.
assertions: Triggers on casual phrasing
Recognizes Medicaid-specific constraints (digital divide, churn)
Proposes channel mix beyond smartphone-only
Addresses SDOH screening and closed-loop referral with verify caveats
Calls out 42 CFR Part 2 and state Medicaid managed-care rules
Proposes preventive-screening outreach (birthday month / care gap)
Stratifies measurement by equity factors
Warns about selection bias in churn analysis

evals.prompt: Can we use Twilio for HIPAA-regulated SMS to patients? And do we need a BAA?
evals.expected_output: Should explain that Twilio offers a BAA (verify current product positioning and which Twilio products are covered — not all are). Should clarify the HIPAA conduit exception is narrow and most messaging platforms that touch message content are not conduits and need a BAA. Should distinguish treatment messages from marketing under TCPA: treatment typically prior express consent on the wireless number; marketing requires prior express written consent. Should call out that the patient should be told SMS is not fully secure and offered a secure-channel alternative. Should not invent specific Twilio product capabilities; should recommend confirming current BAA scope, encryption posture, and any healthcare add-ons directly with Twilio. Should mention state-law overlays (e.g., Florida, Washington) and Part 2 / behavioral-health considerations.
assertions: Confirms BAA is required (conduit exception is narrow)
References that Twilio offers a BAA with verify caveat
Distinguishes treatment vs marketing under TCPA
Notes patient should be told SMS is not fully secure
Does not invent specific Twilio capabilities
Mentions state law overlays and Part 2

evals.prompt: We want to add a chronic-care SMS program for our diabetic patients but our population is heavily older Medicare, many speak Spanish or Vietnamese, and we have a lot of patients with accessibility needs. How do we design for equity?
evals.expected_output: Should call out that an SMS-default program risks excluding the very patients with the highest unmet need. Should propose multi-channel architecture: IVR / human callback alongside SMS; mailed letters where appropriate; portal-with-notify-out for those who use it. Should require language variants (Spanish + Vietnamese at minimum, in-language readability scored separately) and culturally adapted content (defer to health-content-writing). Should require accessibility coverage (defer to accessibility-healthcare): screen-reader-friendly portal, captioned video, TTY / relay alternatives. Should ban race/ethnicity as a segmentation variable without a documented clinical equity rationale. Should propose equity-stratified measurement from day one (not 'add later'), and audit algorithmic prioritization for bias.
assertions: Calls out digital-divide exclusion risk of SMS-default
Proposes multi-channel including IVR / callback / mail
Requires in-language variants with separate readability scoring
Defers cultural adaptation to health-content-writing
Defers accessibility specifics to accessibility-healthcare
Restricts race/ethnicity segmentation
Requires equity-stratified measurement from day one
Calls out algorithmic bias in prioritization

evals.prompt: Can you write the SMS body copy for our diabetes adherence reminder? We need it in English and Spanish, at a 5th-grade reading level.
evals.expected_output: Should recognize that writing the actual content of patient messages — plain language, readability scoring, transcreation to Spanish, cultural adaptation — is the scope of health-content-writing, not patient-engagement. Should defer or hand off to health-content-writing for the content work, while offering to scope the surrounding program (cadence, consent, measurement) if not already done. Should not attempt the full plain-language copywriting using engagement patterns.
assertions: Recognizes content writing is health-content-writing scope
Defers or hands off to health-content-writing
Offers to scope surrounding program if useful
Does not attempt full plain-language copywriting
